finding: Cross-team deployment verification should be represented as one ordered, same-context browser transcript that joins navigation, successful document response, load completion, runtime commit evaluation, and screenshot capture. URL reachability or an isolated screenshot is insufficient.
design_implication: Vercel and Cloudflare verification adapters can emit the same transcript shape, allowing deterministic rejection of mixed contexts, event reordering, unsuccessful document responses, runtime commit mismatches, and missing visual evidence.
evidence_strength: tested prototype contract; not yet integrated with a live WebDriver BiDi implementation
uncertainty: WebDriver BiDi remains a Working Draft and browser implementation support may vary, especially on mobile Safari.
falsification_route: Run an adapter against a deployed site through an actual BiDi-capable browser. Reject this design if conforming event streams cannot be normalized into the contract without browser-specific semantics that alter claim meaning.
trust_limits: A valid transcript is browser-observed evidence, not cryptographic deployment provenance.
A screenshot digest proves exact captured bytes only, not human-visible correctness.
Browser audio state does not prove physical speaker emission or listener perception.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health material, credentials, account identifiers, or user data are included.
